title: Define Structure Worksheet
text: Define Structure Worksheet Use the Define Structure worksheet to create development objects, organize them, and set their properties. Tools Resource Browser—choose templates and other objects to create a new sub-object in the current main development object. Structure Editor—shows the main development object and its sub-objects, as well as selected properties, as a tree. You can edit properties of the selected object here, or in the Properties Inspector. Properties Inspector—shows the properties of the object selected in the Structure Editor, and enables you to edit them. How to Create a New Development Object Add Objects from the Resource Browser Rename a Development Object Edit Object Properties Edit the Properties of Multiple Objects Resize Columns Related Links Using the Uniface IDE Use the Uniface IDE to develop an application, following the Uniface development paradigm of Model, Construct, Build, and Deploy. Working with Development Objects Although development objects can be quite different in content and purpose, the way you create, edit and delete them in the Uniface IDE is essentially the same. Structure Editor The Structure Editor enables you to define the contents of a main development object, including its sub objects. Properties Inspector Use the Properties Inspector to view and edit the properties of the current object, or multiple objects. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
